publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2025-06-25 23:59:55 UTC.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Code for undetermined script script.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Material collected by Rose Marie (Roe) Bressan related to her brother, Arthur J. Bressan, Jr.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Rose Marie Bressan papers document aspects of the personal and family life of the filmmaker Arthur Jr. Bressan and his sister, Rose-Marie Bressan, dating from 1943 to 2019. It contains photographs of their family life, from an early age (including Rose Marie and Artie as infants and college graduation) to adulthood, publicity material, and brochures of festival participations of Arthur's films, after his death in 1987, correspondence between Arthur Jr., Bressan, Rose-Marie Bressan and Vitto Russo, obituary files, personal documents of Arthur Jr. Bressan, such as his passports, cards, his wallet; the documentary "Thank you, Mr. President" (VHS) by Arthur Bressan; two more audiovisual tapes with Rose-Marie Bressan at Astoria Park, family photographs and kids related to Northampton History project (1968). The collection also includes "The Quilt" (book) that features Arthur J. Bressan, Jr.'s first memorial quilt (1987), Rose Marie's album of her visit to the first showing of the quilt in Washington, DC., and photographs of the quilt Rose Marie made later in a 1988 display of the quilt.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Rose Marie "Roe" Bressan is the younger sister of filmmaker Arthur J. Bressan Jr. Since Arthur's death in 1987, Rose-Marie has been a vital steward of his legacy as a pivotal voice in queer independent cinema. She is the co-founder of The Bressan Project, alongside LGBTQ+ film historian and archivist Jenni Olson, established in 2018. The goal of this initiative was to bring Arthur's pioneering work back to screens and platforms, reaching new generations and reaffirming its historical and cultural significance. By partnering with institutions such as the Frameline, UCLA Film & Television Archive, Outfest, and Vinegar Syndrome, Rose-Marie and Jenni have facilitated the 2K restoration and re-release of Arthur's films, including Gay USA (1977), Buddies (1985), and Passing Strangers (1974), Forbidden Letters (1979), Daddy Dearest, and Juice (both 1984). Through her advocacy and collaborations, Rose Marie has played a critical role in keeping her brother Arthur's groundbreaking work visible to new generations.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Related material located in collection 7892, Arthur J. Bressan, Jr. papers.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Rose Marie Bressan papers, #7906. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Arthur Jr. Bressan's death certificate and obituary program

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Handwritten Notes for the Memorial Service by Rosie Marie.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: On set/streets; in Paris, at the New York State Theater; at the beach, playing the bass, and more. Included a photograph from March 8th, 1987.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A year old with his mother; Portraits in different ages; Graduation from Iona College with Alice (fiancée at that time). Iona College TV show "The College Bowl".

did.unittitle: Buddies: festival participations after Arthur Jr. Bressan's death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Buddies: Roe (Rose Marie) Bressan participation as Festival Guest and Panelist at the follwing festivals: Outfest CULA Legacy Project, (July 12-22, 2018), page 50, Out at the movies, International LGBT Film Festival (October 4-7, 2018). included tickets and badges and a film tribute to Arthur J. Bressan by the Castro theatre, San Francisco (August 22, 1987).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence from Arthur Bressan to Rosie-Marie. (1979); Letter from Vitto Russo to Rosie-Marie (1988). Photographs

did.unittitle: Newspaper clippings and other publicity material about Arthur Jr. Bressan's films

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: New York Post, The New York Times, The Village Voice On, Coming Out, Daily News, Variety "Abuse"; Included The Northampton History Project booklet (1968-1969)

did.unittitle: Photographic Album, National March on Washington For Lesbian and Gay Rights

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs with Rose Marie Bressan at the march.

did.unittitle: Photographs from the Quilt (The Names Project)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included photographs from Quilts related to Bressan. Several photographs from differenty Quilts, participants in the event and Rosie Marie's trip to Washington DC for the Quilt.

did.unittitle: Photographs with Arthur Jr. Bressan and his parents

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With parents from six months old to a young boy; Christmas 1952; Communion 1950; Graduation from Iona College with his fiance Alice.

did.unittitle: Programs from Buddies, Gau USA and more after Arthur Jr. Bressan's death

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A note from Peter Sonne about a photograph with Arthur Bressan, Vito Russo, and other friends.

did.unittitle: Rose Marie and Arthur Jr. Bressan photographs

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Included a photograph from the Quilt, Stories from the Names Project in memory of Artie (Arthur) J. Bressan from his sister, Rose Marie, father, and mother (1989), and photographs with Rose Marie, her brotehr Arthur and their parents.

did.unittitle: The Quilt, Stories from The NAMES Project, written by Cindy Ruskin; Photographs by Matt Herron; Design by Deboeah Zemke
VHS "Rose Marie at Astoria Park" and " Lost and Found"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The video title says "Northampton School Class of 1968", but the tape actually is "Rose Marie at Astoria Park"," Lost and Found", Northampton kids from the Northampton History project (1968)

did.unittitle: VHS, documentary: Thank you, Mr. President (VHS) by Arthur Bressan
VHS "Rose Marie at Astoria Park" copy, and "Family photographs of Rose Marie"
